data.title: New Drone Alarm in Denmark: Authorities Suspect 'Hybrid Attack'
data.description:
COPENHAGEN (dpa-AFX) - The Danish government has described the latest drone sightings at several airports across the country as a "hybrid attack." Everything suggests a professional actor is behind the incidents, Defense Minister Troels Lund Poulsen said Thursday morning at a joint press conference with Justice Minister Peter Hummelgaard in Copenhagen. The identity of those responsible remains unclear.

"It is anything but a coincidence that these incidents happened almost simultaneously," Lund Poulsen stated. Rather, it appears to be a systematic operation. "We are talking about something I would define as a hybrid attack involving different types of drones," he said. The acts could pose a threat to security and freedom. However, Lund Poulsen emphasized that there is still no direct military threat against Denmark.

Lund Poulsen said no decision has yet been made on whether to invoke NATO Article 4, which would trigger alliance consultations as other member states have done recently. However, he noted it is an option under consideration.

Aalborg Airport Temporarily Closed

Just two days after a major drone alert at Copenhagen Airport, further drones were spotted late Wednesday evening and into the night over a series of airports in the NATO-member country. At Aalborg Airport in northern Denmark, airspace had to be temporarily closed, resulting in some flights being canceled or diverted.

Drones were also observed near airports in the key port city of Esbjerg and in Sønderborg near the German border, as well as at the Skrydstrup military airbase. According to the responsible police departments, there was no danger to airline passengers or the public.

Just on Monday evening, the sighting of several larger drones led to a more than four-hour closure of Copenhagen Airport, one of Scandinavia's most important hubs. Around 100 flights were canceled, and tens of thousands of passengers were affected by the disruption.

Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen described it as "the most serious attack yet on Danish critical infrastructure." The perpetrators remain unidentified. Frederiksen did not rule out Russian involvement, though the Kremlin has denied any connection. Air traffic at Copenhagen Airport has since returned to normal.

Drone Alarm in the Prime Minister's Hometown

Now, Aalborg Airport--located in Prime Minister Frederiksen's hometown and known for her strong support of Ukraine--also had to be temporarily closed for takeoffs and landings. Since the airport is much smaller than the capital's and the drones were only spotted late Wednesday evening, only a handful of flights were affected. Airspace was reopened after a few hours.

Around 3:00 a.m., police declared the local operation over but announced intensive investigations would follow. Incident commander Jesper Bøjgaard Madsen said authorities were unable to bring down the drones, but believe they are no longer in the area. Flights resumed later in the morning.

Similar Patterns in Aalborg and Copenhagen

The drone activity in Aalborg resembled that in Copenhagen, said Danish National Police Chief Thorkild Fogde at a late-night press conference. "We see a similar pattern: the drones are clearly visible and flying with their lights on," he noted.

Local police reported several drones of a certain size--national police have already ruled out the use of private models. In the Copenhagen incident, police referred to a "capable actor," meaning perpetrators with the necessary skills and tools to carry out such a maneuver--either to create unrest or possibly just to test how long their drones could remain undetected in the air.

Further Sightings in Western Denmark

The first drones in Aalborg were spotted around 9:44 p.m.--almost simultaneously, similar sightings were reported in Esbjerg, Sønderborg, and Skrydstrup. All these locations, like Aalborg, are in Jutland, the western part of Denmark bordering Germany to the south. Police said the drones were flying with their lights on and were visible from the ground. Unlike Aalborg, the other airports did not have to be closed, as no flights were scheduled until morning.

Unclear Situation in Billund

As if that were not enough, Billund Airport in the small town of Billund was also briefly closed for nearly an hour overnight. The reasons remain unclear--reports of possible drone activity have yet to be confirmed. The town is best known as the home of Danish toy giant LEGO./trs/DP/jha

data.title: JD Health, the pharmacy without walls that is conquering China
data.description:
A unique Internet + healthcare model

JD Health does not define itself solely as a digital pharmacy. Its mission is to integrate the entire healthcare value chain through an Internet + healthcare model that combines three pillars:

  • Pharmaceutical distribution: over 30 new innovative drugs launched in H1 2025, covering therapeutic areas as varied as hypertension, obesity, and oncology.
  • Online medical services: daily consultations exceeding 500,000 interactions, with five key specialties (dermatology, mental health, oncology, andrology and traditional Chinese medicine).
  • Management and prevention solutions: centers dedicated to nutrition, weight management, and rare diseases, reinforced by AI-powered intelligent agents.

This Retail Pharmacy + Healthcare Service ecosystem forms a closed loop where users can move from consultation to diagnosis, then to purchase and delivery of treatment, all without leaving the platform.

Exemplary financial growth

In H1 2025, JD Health posted revenue of RMB 35.3bn, up 24.5% y-o-y. Sales of pharmaceutical and healthcare products accounted for the bulk of this (RMB 29.3bn, +22.7%), but it was online services that were particularly impressive, growing 34.4% to RMB 6bn. Profitability improved significantly, with its gross margin reaching 25.2% (compared to 23.6% a year earlier), thanks to a more favorable product mix and logistical efficiency gains. Net profit reached RMB 2.6bn, up nearly 28%. Reflecting its financial discipline, JD Health maintains a debt-free balance sheet and abundant cash reserves, with over
RMB 66bn in liquid assets at the end of June 2025.

Competitive advantage: technology and scale

In a highly competitive market dominated by Alibaba Health, Ping An Good Doctor, and Meituan, JD Health stands out thanks to two key drivers:

  • The logistical power inherited from JD.com: a network of 200,000 connected pharmacies capable of ensuring almost instant delivery. This O2O (online-to-offline) capability makes the promise of accessible and rapid care a reality for nearly 200 million Chinese people.
  • The integration of artificial intelligence: more than 50 million users have already used AI Jingyi smart medical agents. These virtual assistants optimize care, improve diagnostic accuracy, and increase patient loyalty.

This technological advantage provides a defensive barrier against competition and a monetization lever that is still under-exploited.

A market driven by Beijing and household demand

The macroeconomic environment is favorable for JD Health. The government's Healthy China 2030 plan aims to grow the healthcare market to
RMB 16 trillion by 2030. Public support for digital technology and AI in healthcare is accelerating the migration to online services. Consumer demand is equally dynamic, with weight management, personalized nutrition, and home care experiencing rapid growth. The company has already positioned itself in these segments through strategic partnerships, notably with Novo Nordisk in diabetes and obesity, and Roche in infectious disease prevention.

Societal impact and brand image

Beyond the numbers, JD Health is strengthening its legitimacy by getting involved in public health. The company has supported more than 31,000 patients with rare diseases, distributed more than 170,000 medical products during the Shigatse earthquake, and participated in national screening programs. This commitment fosters a positive brand image, a key factor in a sector where patient trust is essential.

Outlook and valuation

We anticipate an acceleration in the penetration of online pharmacies, with market share potentially reaching 32% in 2030, up from the previous estimate of 21%. In this context, JD Health appears to be best positioned to capture this growth thanks to its integrated model. Revenue forecasts have been raised to RMB 70bn in 2025, then to more than RMB 91bn in 2027, with average annual growth of around 15% to 20%. At a multiple of 45x 2025 earnings and 38x 2026 earnings, the valuation reflects a growth stock, but the combination of market size, earnings momentum, and AI monetization potential suggests significant upside potential.

For these reasons, we have added the company to MarketScreener's Asia-Pacific Investor portfolio with the management team, with an initial purchase on September 3, 2025, at a price of HKD 64.4, representing 5.5% of the portfolio.

data.title: Snam CEO 'amazed' by lengthy approval for German gas grid deal, report says
data.description:
MILAN (Reuters) -Italy's Snam was amazed by the lengthy approval process it faces over its acquisition of a stake in Germany's largest independent gas transmission operator, its CEO told an Italian newspaper, hinting the group could pull the deal.

The Italian gas grid operator signed a deal in April to buy 24.99% in Open Grid Europe, paving the way for Snam to enter the German gas market.

"The transaction is proceeding... but we are amazed by the lengthiness of the process due to the incredible amount of additional documents requested by the German authority," Snam's CEO Agostino Scornajenchi told daily Il Sole 24 Ore.

The asset is interesting, but Snam would not pursue the acquisition "come hell or high water," Scornajenchi added.

Part of the information requested by the German authority was related to the role of China's State Grid, an indirect investor in Snam, sources told Reuters.

Germany's economy ministry, which is reviewing Snam's acquisition, declined to comment.

China's State Grid owns 35% in CDP Reti, which in turns holds 31.4% of Snam.

In 2018, Germany blocked an attempt by China's State Grid to buy a stake in power grid operator 50Hertz.

($1 = 0.8510 euros)

(Reporting by Francesca Landini in Milan, additional reporting Christoph Steitz in Frankfurt; Editing by Bernadette Baum)

data.title: Blackstone weighs options for Ancestry.com, including sale or IPO, sources say
data.description:
NEW YORK (Reuters) -Blackstone is weighing strategic options, including an initial public offering or a sale, for Ancestry.com, the hugely popular platform that helps users trace their genealogical roots, people familiar with the matter said.

The buyout firm invited banks to pitch for an IPO of the Lehi, Utah-based company, the people said, asking not to be identified because the information is not public. Requests for proposals were submitted by the banks earlier this week, they added.

A public listing could value the company at about $10 billion, the people said, cautioning that discussions are at an early stage and details are subject to change. The sources also said that Blackstone could consider selling the asset, but no additional plans could be learned about this option.

Deliberations are ongoing and Blackstone could decide to hold onto the asset for longer, the people said. 

Representatives from Blackstone and Ancestry.com declined to comment.

The deliberations come as the U.S. IPO market has rebounded since early September after a slow second quarter. First-time share sales this year have already surpassed those in 2024, according to LSEG data.

Ancestry.com generates more than $1 billion in annual revenue and has over 3 million paying subscribers, according to its website. The company makes money primarily from subscriptions to its historical records and family trees, as well as sales of DNA testing kits.

Blackstone acquired the company in 2020 for $4.7 billion from a group of private equity firms, while Singapore's sovereign wealth fund GIC retained a minority stake.

Founded in 1996 as Ancestry Publishing, the company has changed hands several times. In 2016, Silver Lake Partners and GIC bought minority stakes at a $2.6 billion enterprise value. In 2012, Permira, Spectrum Equity and Ancestry management took the company private in a $1.6 billion deal.

(Reporting by Echo Wang in New York; additional reporting by Abigail Summerville; Editing by Anna Driver)

By Echo Wang

data.title: Indian refiners' August crude processing drops 4.4% from a month earlier
data.description:
Sept 25 (Reuters) - Indian refiners' crude throughput declined 4.4%
month-on-month in August to 5.27 million barrels per day (22.29 million metric
tons), according to provisional government data released on Thursday.
    Refinery throughput in July was at 5.51 million barrels per day (23.31
million metric tons). 
    On a year-on-year basis, refinery throughput rose 3% in August.
    India's fuel consumption in August hit an 11-month low, slipping 3.8%
month-on-month to 18.73 million metric tons, oil ministry data showed. 
    India is the world's third-biggest oil importer and consumer.
        Meanwhile, Indian oil refiners are increasing gasoline and diesel
exports to their highest levels in several years, driven by expanded crude
processing capacity and increased domestic ethanol blending that has freed up
fuel supplies for overseas markets, traders and analysts said.
    This year, India's crude processing is expected to increase by 130,000 to
160,000 barrels per day to about 5.51 million bpd, with gasoline exports hitting
a record high of around 400,000 bpd, according to consultancy Wood Mackenzie.
    In July, European Union countries approved an 18th sanctions package against
Russia over its war in Ukraine, with a lower price cap on Russian oil. 
    The rise in Indian exports is expected to help meet Europe's winter heating
oil demand and support Indian refining margins, after refiners turned to
discounted Russian crude.

data.title: Starbucks to close stores, cut more jobs as CEO deepens restructuring
data.description:
(Reuters) -Starbucks said on Thursday it has approved a restructuring plan to close underperforming coffee houses in North America, and cut some jobs as the company looks to revive sales and profits under CEO Brian Niccol.

The company has been overhauling its operations in the United States, centered around Niccol's plan to bring back a coffeehouse environment at its stores by introducing ceramic mugs, comfortable seating and reduced wait times.

"During the review, we identified coffeehouses where we're unable to create the physical environment our customers and partners expect, or where we don't see a path to financial performance, and these locations will be closed," Niccol said in a letter to employees.

Starbucks would also cut about 900 jobs in its support teams and close many open positions, he added.

The company expects to incur about $1 billion in costs under the restructuring plan, it said in a regulatory filing.

(Reporting by Juveria Tabassum in Bengaluru; Editing by Leroy Leo)

data.title: Jabil Fiscal Q4 Core Earnings, Net Revenue Rise; Fiscal Q1, 2026 Outlook Issued
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Jabil (JBL) reported fiscal Q4 core earnings Thursday of $3.29 per diluted share, up from $2.30 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $2.92.

Net revenue for the quarter ended Aug. 31 was $8.25 billion, compared with $6.96 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $7.59 billion.

For fiscal 2026, the company said it expects core earnings of $11 per diluted share on revenue of $31.3 billion.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect core earnings of $10.81 on revenue of $30.71 billion.

For fiscal Q1, the company said it expects core earnings in the range of $2.47 to $2.87 per share on net revenue of $7.7 billion to $8.3 billion.

Analysts polled by FactSet expect $2.40 and $7.53 billion, respectively.

Shares of the company were down 3.7% in recent premarket activity Thursday.

data.title: Poly Medicure to Expand Global Orthopaedics Footprint With Medistream Acquisition
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Poly Medicure (NSE:POLYMED, BOM:531768) has signed a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of Medistream, including Citieffe SRL and its subsidiaries in the USA and Mexico, at an enterprise value of 3.24 billion Indian rupees, according to a Thursday filing.

Citieffe, an Italy-based manufacturer in the orthopaedic trauma and extremities segment, has direct operations in Italy, USA, and Mexico, with distribution in over 25 countries.

Existing management, including CEO Pascal Govi, will continue, the filing said.

The transaction is expected to close in 4-8 weeks, subject to customary approvals.

Shares of the company were up nearly 3% in after-market hours.

data.title: Cipher Mining Signs 10-Year AI hosting Deal with Fluidstack; Alphabet's Google backstops $1.40 billion
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Cipher Mining (CIFR) said Thursday that it has signed a 10-year high-performance computing colocation agreement with Fluidstack to deliver 168 MW of critical IT load at its Barber Lake site in Colorado City, Texas, by September of next year.

The US data center company said the agreement secures $3 billion in contracted revenue over the initial term and could reach $7 billion with two five-year extensions under a modified gross lease, Cipher said.

Alphabet's Google (GOOG, GOOGL) will backstop $1.40 billion of Fluidstack's lease obligations to support project debt and will receive warrants to acquire 24 million Cipher shares, or a 5.4% pro forma stake, Cipher said.

The Barber Lake plan includes up to 244 MW of gross capacity on 587 acres, with the option of expanding to 500 MW, and project costs ranging from $9 to $11 million per MW of critical IT load, the company said.

Cipher said it plans to retain full ownership of the project, may access capital markets to fund a portion of the project, and will continue to prioritize a 2.4 GW HPC pipeline.

Shares of the company were up more than 9% in recent Thursday premarket activity.

data.title: Bristol Myers Squibb to Offer Discounted Plaque Psoriasis Medicine
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller


Bristol Myers Squibb said it would offer its Sotyktu drug for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis for 80% less than its current list price to eligible patients.

The initiative expands on the company's discounted program for blood clot drug Eliquis, which was announced in July amid pressure on the pharmaceutical industry from the Trump administration to cut drug prices.

Eligible cash-pay U.S. patients with a Sotyktu prescription will be able purchase the drug at a discount through the company's patient connect platform. Bristol Myers will begin to offer Sotyktu through the new platform in January.


Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

09-25-25 0755ET

data.title: Turkish competition authority launches probe into Spotify
data.description:
ISTANBUL (Reuters) -Turkey's competition authority said on Thursday that it has launched an investigation into Spotify to look into possible discriminatory practices and predatory pricing in the online music streaming market.

The probe will examine whether Spotify favors certain rights holders in its algorithms, and whether its subscription pricing in Turkey is set at levels that unfairly hinder competitors and rights holders, the antitrust authority said on its website.

(Reporting by Ezgi Erkoyun; Editing by Daren Butler)

data.title: US Dollar Rises Early Thursday Ahead of Busy Data, Fed Appearance Schedule
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- The US dollar rose against its major trading partners early Thursday, except for decline versus the Canadian dollar, before a busy day of economic data releases and appearances by Federal Reserve officials.

Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee is due to speak at 8:20 am ET, followed by a quartet of data releases at 8:30 am ET - weekly jobless claims, durable goods orders and advance trade data for August, and an update to Q2 gross domestic product growth that will include annual benchmark revisions.

New York Fed President John Williams and Kansas City Fed President Jeffrey Schmid are both expected to speak at 9:00 am ET, followed by existing-home sales data for August at 10:00 am ET, at the same time as an appearance by Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman.

Weekly natural gas stocks inventory data are due to be released at 10:30 am ET and Kansas City manufacturing data for September are due at 11:00 am ET.

Fed Governor Michael Barr is scheduled to speak at 1:00 pm ET, followed by Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan at 1:40 pm ET and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly at 3:30 pm ET.

A quick summary of foreign exchange activity heading into Thursday:

EUR/USD fell to 1.1737 from 1.1739 at the Wednesday US close and 1.1751 at the same time Wednesday morning. Eurozone private sector loan growth accelerated in August, according to data released earlier Thursday. The next European Central Bank meeting is scheduled for Oct. 30.

GBP/USD fell to 1.3434 from 1.3446 at the Wednesday US close and 1.3468 at the same time Wednesday morning. UK car registrations declined sharply in August, according to data released overnight. The next Bank of England meeting is scheduled for Nov. 6.

USD/JPY rose to 148.8741 from 148.8556 at the Wednesday US close and 148.4167 at the same time Wednesday morning. Japanese corporate services price rose at a faster pace in August than in the previous month, according to data released overnight. The next Bank of Japan meeting is scheduled for Oct. 29.

USD/CAD fell to 1.3894 from 1.3901 at the Wednesday US close but was above a level of 1.3879 at the same time Wednesday morning. Canadian average weekly earnings data for July are due to be released at 8:30 am ET. The next Bank of Canada meeting is scheduled for Oct. 29.

data.title: Tech Fade, Geopolitcs Dampen European Bourse Midday
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- European bourses tracked moderately lower midday Thursday after the bloom left the global tech rally, and as Ukraine-Russian tensions mounted.

Retail stocks led gainers, while bank issues lagged in continental trading.

Investors also viewed soft Wall Street futures, and choppy closes overnight on Asian exchanges.

In economic news, Swiss National Bank (SNB) Governing Board Chairman Martin Schlegel said US tariffs challenge the Swiss economy. The central bank held its key interest rate unchanged at 0%, after a policy session.

In other news, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky told "The Axios Show" that if Russia won't end the war, Kremlin officials should learn the location of nearby bomb shelters.

The pan-continental Stoxx Europe 600 Index was off 0.4% mid-session.

The Stoxx Europe 600 Technology Index was down 0.3%, and the Stoxx 600 Banks Index lost 0.6%.

The Stoxx Europe 600 Oil and Gas Index was off 0.4%, but the Stoxx 600 Europe Food and Beverage Index rose 0.3%.

The REITE, a European REIT index, fell 0.1%, while the Stoxx Europe 600 Retail Index was up 0.9%.

On the national market indexes, Germany's DAX was down 1%, and the FTSE 100 in London lost 0.4%. The CAC 40 in Paris was off 0.7%, and Spain's IBEX 35 eased 0.2%.

Yields on benchmark 10-year German bonds were lower, near 2.76%.

Front-month North Sea Brent crude-oil futures were down 0.3% at $69.08 a barrel.

The Euro Stoxx 50 volatility index was up 1.2% to 16.99, still indicating below-average volatility for European stock markets in the next 30 days, a positive signal. A reading above 20 indicates choppier markets ahead, while below 20 suggests calmer exchanges.

data.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb to Offer Sotyktu, Eliquis Drugs at 'Steeply Discounted Prices' to Eligible US Patients
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) said Thursday it is offering its Sotyktu and Eliquis drugs to eligible US patients at "steeply discounted prices" in an expansion of its direct-to-patient offerings.

Sotyktu, the company's drug treating moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis, will be offered through its new BMS Patient Connect platform at an over 80% discount to the current list price starting January 2026, Bristol-Myers said.

The Bristol-Myers Squibb-Pfizer (PFE) Alliance program for Eliquis, a treatment for venous thromboembolism, was launched earlier in September, with a discount of over 40% to the list price offered to cash-paying patients, the company said.

The move comes around two months after President Donald Trump sent letters to global drug manufacturers demanding that they price new medicines in the US similar to how they charge other wealthy nations and set up direct-to-consumer channels to sell some popular treatments at a discount, according to a Bloomberg News report Thursday.

data.title: Sino Biopharm Completes First Patient Enrollment of Anti-Tumor Drug Trial in Australia
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Sino Biopharmaceutical (HKG:1177) completed the enrollment of the first patient in a phase I clinical trial of LM-350, developed by unit LaNova Medicines, in Australia, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The study aims to evaluate the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetic characteristics, and preliminary efficacy of the drug in patients with advanced solid tumors.

data.title: Rheinmetall to Build Ammunition Factory in Latvia
data.description:
Europe's largest ammunition producer, Rheinmetall, is set to expand its production capacity by constructing a new plant in Latvia, a move aimed at bolstering the Baltic nation's defense capabilities.

In the presence of Latvian Prime Minister Evika Silina, a corresponding memorandum of understanding (MoU) was signed Thursday in Hamburg, Rheinmetall announced. The facility will be operated as a joint venture, with Rheinmetall Waffe Munition GmbH holding a 51 percent stake and the Latvian State Defence Corporation holding 49 percent. The plant, which is projected to have an annual capacity of several tens of thousands of artillery shells, will see an investment of 275 million euros in Latvia and is expected to create around 150 jobs. By establishing its own 155mm ammunition production, Latvia is taking another step to strengthen its defense capabilities.

The DAX-listed group is responding to increased demand from the German Bundeswehr, other Western armies, and Ukrainian forces in light of the growing threat from Russia. Previously, Rheinmetall announced plans to produce a total of around 1.5 million artillery shells per year starting in 2027.

"With this production facility, we are expanding our position as the world's leading manufacturer of artillery ammunition," said Rheinmetall CEO Armin Papperger. "I am confident that we can establish comparable partnerships for ammunition production in other countries as well." Silina stated, "The signing of this memorandum of understanding is a clear step toward strengthening Latvia's security and developing our defense industry."

At the end of August, Rheinmetall inaugurated a new plant in Unterlüß, Lower Saxony, which, when fully operational, is set to become the largest ammunition factory in Europe. The group also manufactures artillery shells in Spain. Similar facilities are planned for Lithuania, Romania, and Bulgaria, with a plant in Ukraine also under consideration.

The Düsseldorf-based company is strategically expanding its production capacities in response to Russia's war against Ukraine. Rheinmetall supplies Ukraine with weapons and ammunition, and the arms boom has resulted in rapid growth for the company. Rheinmetall also plans to enter warship construction by acquiring the military division of Bremen's Lürssen Group. The group is relying on partnerships to meet surging demand: together with Leonardo, Rheinmetall aims to build tanks in Italy, and it is working with US company Lockheed Martin on rockets and fighter jets.

(Reporting by Matthias Inverardi, edited by Myria Mildenberger. For inquiries, please contact our editorial team at berlin.newsroom@thomsonreuters.com (for politics and economics) or frankfurt.newsroom@thomsonreuters.com (for companies and markets).

data.title: Sun Life Financial Appoints Sujoy Ghosh as CEO of High Net Worth Unit, Effective from November 4, 2025
data.description:
Sun Life Financial Inc. announced the appointment of Sujoy Ghosh as Chief Executive Officer of High Net Worth or HNW business from November 4. He will lead the global operations of the business in Hong Kong, Singapore and Bermuda. The new HNW unit head will report to Manjit Singh, President, Sun Life Asia, and be based in Singapore. Ghosh, with over 27 years of total experience in insurance and banking, most recently served as the Head of Global Insurance & Asia Head Business Development at Citi Wealth.

Prior to Citi, he had held Regional Head of Bancassurance for Allianz in Asia Pacific, handling commercial and strategic partnerships across Life and General Insurance.

data.title: Recruit Holdings Co., Ltd. authorizes a Buyback Plan.
data.description: The Board of Directors of Recruit Holdings Co., Ltd. has authorized a buyback plan on September 25, 2025.

data.title: Evaxion Licenses EVX-B3 Vaccine Candidate to Merck
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher


Evaxion has licensed its EVX-B3 vaccine candidate to Merck & Co. in a deal potentially worth more than $600 million to the clinical-stage biotechnology company.

Evaxion on Thursday said Merck exercised its option to license EVX-B3 in exchange for an upfront payment of $7.5 million, which extends the Denmark-based company's cash runway to the first half of 2027.

Evaxion, which has been working with Merck since September 2023, said it is also eligible for up to $592 million in development, regulatory and sales milestone payments, along with royalties on product sales.

Rahway, N.J., drugmaker Merck will assume full responsibility and carry all costs for the further development of the EVX-B3 vaccine, which is currently in preclinical development and targets a bacterial pathogen.

Evaxion said it has agreed with Merck to extend the evaluation period for EVX-B2, a preclinical vaccine candidate against gonorrhea, with a decision on licensing by Merck expected in the first half of 2026.

Shares of Evaxion, which closed Wednesday at $3.30, were recently up 24% to $4.10 in premarket trading.


Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

09-25-25 0749ET

data.title: Recruit Holdings Co., Ltd. announces an Equity Buyback for 15,000,000 shares, representing 1.05% for ¥130,000 million.
data.description:
Recruit Holdings Co., Ltd. (TSE:6098) announces a share repurchase program. Under the program, the company will repurchase up to 15,000,000 shares, representing 1.05% of its total shares outstanding (excluding treasury shares), for a total of ¥130,000 million. The shares will be repurchased from ¥8,022 per share.

The purpose of repurchase program is to maximize shareholder returns. The program will be funded using company?s own funds. The purpose of the program is to invest in its long-term business strategy in order to achieve sustainable profit growth and increase enterprise value, to further improve capital efficiency and to maximize shareholder returns.

The shares acquired through the Purchases may be used in the future for the delivery of shares upon the exercise of stock acquisition rights, for stock compensation using the Company?s common stock for employees of the Group (the Company and its subsidiaries), or for strategic M&A with the Company?s common stock as consideration, as well as may be cancelled. As of August 31, 2025, the company had 1,430,210,526 shares outstanding (excluding treasury shares) and had 133,701,623 shares in treasury.

data.description: ABF Pan Asia Bond Index Fund reported earnings results for the full year ended June 30, 2025. For the full year, the company reported revenue was USD 442.45 million compared to USD 81.8 million a year ago. Net income was USD 326.36 million compared to net loss of USD 37.43 million a year ago.

data.title: Accenture beats revenue estimates, plans $865 million restructuring amid AI shift
data.description:
(Reuters) -Accenture on Thursday reported fourth-quarter revenue slightly above Wall Street estimates, driven by resilient demand for its artificial intelligence-driven consulting and services from enterprise clients.

The company posted fourth-quarter revenue of $17.6 billion, compared with estimates of $17.36 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

(Reporting by Kritika Lamba in Bengaluru: Editing by Tasim Zahid)

data.title: Drugs Made In America Acquisition II Prices IPO at $10 per Unit
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Drugs Made In America Acquisition II (DMII) said Thursday it has priced an initial public offering of 50 million units at $10.00 per unit.

Each unit consists of one share and a right to one-tenth (1/10) of a share upon the consummation of an initial business combination, the blank-check company said.

The units are expected to be listed on Nasdaq and trade under the ticker symbol "DMIIU" beginning Sept. 25, the company said.

Once the units begin separate trading, the shares and rights are expected to be listed under the symbols "DMII" and "DMIIR," respectively, the company said.

The offering is expected to close on Friday.

The company also said that it has granted underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 7.5 million units to cover over-allotments, if any.

data.title: White House asks for mass firing plans ahead of possible government shutdown
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -The White House asked federal agencies on Wednesday to prepare plans for mass firings during a possible government shutdown next week, marking a sharp departure from the temporary furloughs of workers typically seen during past shutdowns. 

The White House's Office of Management and Budget (OMB) sent the memo to federal agencies and asked them to identify programs, projects and activities where discretionary funding will lapse on October 1 if the U.S. Congress does not pass legislation to keep the federal government open.

"Programs that did not benefit from an infusion of mandatory appropriations will bear the brunt of a shutdown," the OMB said in the memo, which the White House provided.

It was not clear whether the White House was trying to take advantage of a possible shutdown to advance President Donald Trump's push to slash the federal workforce, or whether it was a negotiating tactic to force Democrats to agree to pass the Republicans' funding legislation.

"This is an attempt at intimidation," Senate Democratic leader Chuck Schumer said in a statement late Wednesday. "Donald Trump has been firing federal workers since Day One - not to govern, but to scare."

He predicted any firings will be overturned in court, as others have been.

Agencies were told to submit their proposed reduction-in-force plans to the OMB and to issue notices to employees.

The OMB memo was earlier reported by Politico.

Trump on Tuesday scrapped a meeting with top congressional Democratic leaders to discuss government funding, raising the risk of a partial government shutdown next week. Republicans and Democrats have blamed each other for the impasse.

Upon taking office in January, Trump launched a campaign to downsize the 2.4 million-member federal civilian workforce, which he says is bloated and inefficient.

Roughly 300,000 federal civilian workers will have left their jobs by the end of 2025, Office of Personnel Management Director Scott Kupor told Reuters in August.

About 154,000 of those employees accepted a buyout and are slated to drop off the U.S. government's payroll on September 30, the last day of the federal government's fiscal year. That date is also the deadline for Trump and Congress to reach an agreement on federal spending to avert a shutdown.

(Reporting by Kanishka Singh, Ismail Shakil and Courtney Rozen; Editing by Jasper Ward, Ross Colvin, Christian Schmollinger and Chizu Nomiyama)

By Kanishka Singh

data.title: Starbucks Flags Store Closures, Job Cuts Under Restructuring Plan
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Starbucks (SBUX) said in a Thursday filing that its board has approved a restructuring plan the will see the closure of a number of its coffeehouses and a reduction of about 900 current non-retail partner roles in the company.

Chief Executive Officer Brian Niccol announced the job cuts in a letter issued the same day, saying affected employees will be notified on Friday. Niccol said the company is also closing "many open positions."

The company did not specify the number of store closures but Niccol said Starbucks' overall company-owned store count in North America will decline by 1% in fiscal 2025 after accounting for the year's store openings and closures.

Starbucks said it expects to complete majority of the store closures related to the restructuring by the end of this fiscal year. The company estimates to incur about $1 billion in costs related to the closures, support organization transformation, and other restructuring activities, with majority of which to be incurred in fiscal 2025.

data.title: Rheinmetall to Build Ammunition Plant in Latvia -- Update
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo


Rheinmetall plans to build an ammunition plant in Latvia, as part of its efforts to ramp up production to respond to increasing demand from North Atlantic Treaty Organization's member states.

The German arms maker signed a preliminary deal in Hamburg on Thursday for the construction of a new 155mm ammunition factory in the Baltic country, helping Latvia to boost its deterrence vis-a-vis Russia.

The plant will significantly strengthen the security of ammunition supplies in the Baltic region and Northern Europe, Latvia's Prime Minister Evika Silina said in a statement.

Rheinmetall will own 51% of the shares in the factory, with the remainder being held by Latvia's State Defence Corp, the company said, adding that it will invest about 275 million euros ($322.8 million) in the plant and create about 150 jobs.

The agreement comes after the DAX-listed company entered a similar arrangement with Lithuania last year.

Last month, Rheinmetall opened another plant in the German state of Lower Saxony, and has floated plans to build additional ones in Bulgaria, Ukraine and Romania.

Demand for ammunition is being driven by Europe's large donations to help Ukraine fight back Russia's invasion, and the continent's need to replenish its own stockpiles, which Rheinmetall's Chief Executive Armin Papperger predicts would take a decade.

Rheinmetall's weapons and ammunition business hit record sales of 1.32 billion euros in the first half of fiscal 2025, up 26% on year.


Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

09-25-25 0744ET

data.title: PRESS DIGEST-Canada - September 25
data.description:
Sept 25 - The following are the top stories from selected Canadian newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

THE GLOBE AND MAIL

** A former Royal Bank of Canada employee is facing criminal charges after allegedly accessing Prime Minister Mark Carney's banking profile and participating in a fraud scheme believed to be linked to organized crime.

** The federal government is launching a task force on artificial intelligence and will deliver an updated national strategy to support and develop the sector by the end of year, AI Minister Evan Solomon says.

** Equifax Canada, one of two main credit bureaus in Canada, announced on Thursday the rollout of a new AI-powered tool it says will provide consumers with personalized tips to help them improve their credit scores. (Compiled by Bengaluru newsroom)

data.title: Rheinmetall to Develop New Artillery Ammunition Production Facility in Latvia
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Rheinmetall (RHM.F) plans to develop a new production facility in Latvia to produce 155-millimeter caliber artillery ammunition, according to a Wednesday release.

Under a corresponding memorandum of understanding, the facility will involve a 275 million-euro investment. It will be operated by a joint venture entity 51%-owned by Rheinmetall Waffe Munition and the Latvian State Defence holding the remaining 49%. The ammunition will supply the National Armed Forces of Latvia and the company's partner countries.

The German arms maker said it will start construction in the spring of 2026, with production expected in 2027.

data.title: Toyota Launches Woven City Real World Test Site in Japan
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Toyota Motor (TM) said Thursday it has officially launched Toyota Woven City in Susono City, Japan, as a real-world testing ground for mobility.

The project is developed jointly with Woven by Toyota and was first announced in 2020.

Toyota said residents have begun moving in, while partner companies have started co-creation activities. Participants include enterprises, startups, entrepreneurs, and research institutions developing products and services, alongside residents who will experience the products and services first-hand and providing feedback, Toyota said.

data.title: Swiss regulator sees progress on UBS crisis planning, but work still to be done
data.description:
ZURICH (Reuters) -UBS has made progress on its crisis planning but authorities need to have more options for tackling an emergency at the bank, Switzerland's financial market regulator FINMA said on Thursday.

Last October, FINMA said UBS needed to improve its emergency and recovery plans following the bank's government-engineered takeover of Credit Suisse, which collapsed in March 2023.

Since then, the bank's ability to restructure or wind up the business had improved, and UBS had made progress in remedying previously identified shortcomings, FINMA said.

The regulator said the bank's emergency plan - which sets out how systemically important functions would be maintained if broader efforts to revive or wind up the bank were unsuccessful - largely fulfils current statutory requirements.

But that emergency plan still needs to be better integrated in the resolution plan in the future and thus cannot currently be regarded as executable, FINMA said.

The regulator added there must be "greater optionality" for authorities, requiring legislative changes. The government has proposed stricter measures for UBS to avoid a repeat of the Credit Suisse meltdown and to shield taxpayers from losses.

UBS said it met the current requirements to be resolved under the preferred resolution strategy in the event of a crisis. As an additional safeguard, the bank said, the Swiss emergency plan would be available in an extreme scenario.

That plan is designed in line with legal requirements to ensure continuity and protection of systemically important functions in Switzerland, UBS said in a statement.

(Reporting by Dave Graham. Editing by Mark Potter, Kirsten Donovan)

data.title: Blue Owl Forms Digital Infrastructure Platform Partnership With Qatar Investment Authority
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Blue Owl Capital (OWL) and Qatar Investment Authority said Thursday they have formed a partnership to create a digital infrastructure platform with more than $3 billion of initial data center assets.

The partnership aims to expand global compute capacity for hyperscalers and meet growing demand for cloud and artificial intelligence infrastructure, the firms said.
